methodsAttempted.query: site:unimi.it (AI OR "artificial intelligence" OR "intelligenza artificiale" OR ChatGPT OR generative) (policy OR guidance OR guidelines OR didattica OR esami OR studenti)
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search found the official University of Milan AI governance page and three official PDF annexes for education, research, and administrative activities.
Verified all used sources are hosted on www.unimi.it, the University of Milan official domain, and fetched without login or browser escalation.

methodsAttempted.query: site:air.unimi.it generative artificial intelligence regulations University of Milan
methodsAttempted.notes: An institutional repository article was visible in search results but was rejected as research literature rather than University policy or guidance.

artifacts.summary: Official-source discovery located a University of Milan AI governance page and three official guideline annexes for education, research, and administrative activities. Evidence was taken only from the official HTML page and official PDF annexes on www.unimi.it.

stopConditions: Stop after official AI governance page and official annex PDFs support narrow claims.
Do not infer binding university-wide academic discipline rules beyond the wording in the AI education guidelines.
Do not use search snippets, repository scholarship, news, vendor pages, or social media as evidence.
Do not stage raw PDFs, HTML, screenshots, or full normalized text in Git.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Guidelines for Beneficial Use of AI in Educational Settings
Guidelines for Beneficial Use of AI in Research
Guidelines for Beneficial Use of AI in Administrative Activities

artifacts.claimText: The University of Milan has an AI governance document intended to promote ethical, legally compliant and conscious use of AI tools, with annexed guidelines for teaching, research, third mission and administrative activities.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: The goal of the document "Governing Artificial Intelligence within the University" is to promote an ethical, legally-compliant and conscious use of AI tools, by implementing the latest regulatory provisions on the subject, as well as best practices already adopted by many universities and research centres in Italy and abroad. The ten general principles stated in this document are accompanied by three Annexes containing specific guidelines for the use of AI in the areas of teaching, research and the third mission, and administrative activities.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Main body text under Governing AI within the University
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: In educational settings, the University of Milan advises that students must explicitly declare AI use in assessed work and that teachers must explicitly declare AI use in educational activities.

citation.citationTitle: Guidelines for Beneficial Use of AI in Educational Settings
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Guidelines for Beneficial Use of AI in Educational Settings
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: For teachers: any use of AI tools in educational activities must be made explicit and declared, both regarding the specific tool used and the methods of use. For students: any use of AI tools in producing work subject to assessment must be made explicit and declared, specifying the methods of use and purposes.
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

citation.citationTitle: Guidelines for Beneficial Use of AI in Educational Settings
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: For educational activities, the University of Milan permits AI-supported self-assessment when explicit, but does not permit delegating learning assessment to AI tools when the assessment contributes to the final exam grade.

citation.citationTitle: Guidelines for Beneficial Use of AI in Educational Settings
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Guidelines for Beneficial Use of AI in Educational Settings
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: It is never permitted to delegate any learning assessment activity to AI tools when such activity involves an assessment that contributes to the final exam grade. Self-assessment aimed at improving learning is instead permitted and promoted provided it is made explicit in the tools and methods of use.
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

citation.citationTitle: Guidelines for Beneficial Use of AI in Educational Settings
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: For research, the University of Milan advises research staff to use AI while respecting responsibility, transparency, and respect, and says researchers remain fully responsible for results produced with AI tools.

citation.citationTitle: Guidelines for Beneficial Use of AI in Research
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Guidelines for Beneficial Use of AI in Research
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: This is why the University advises its research staff to learn about, exploit, and systematically implement the best existing tools while, at the same time, urging them to always do so while respecting three fundamental principles that must characterize the entire process of using these tools: responsibility, transparency, and respect. Throughout the entire research process... the researcher... must at all times understand that they are fully responsible for all results produced by the tools used, regardless of AI use or not.
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

citation.citationTitle: Guidelines for Beneficial Use of AI in Research
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: For AI use in research, the University of Milan guidance emphasizes data protection, operation traceability and regulatory compliance during AI-supported data analysis, and cautions against entering sensitive information into AI systems.

citation.citationTitle: Guidelines for Beneficial Use of AI in Research
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Guidelines for Beneficial Use of AI in Research
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: In any case, data protection, operation traceability, and regulatory compliance must be guaranteed throughout the entire analysis process. The idea of respect then includes the need to correctly treat information entered into AI systems by respecting both privacy and data protection. Avoiding inserting particularly sensitive data into AI systems achieves both the objective of protecting the University's industrial secrets... and the rights and freedoms of people to whom any personal and sensitive data may refer.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Use of AI systems for data analysis; Section 2.3
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

citation.citationTitle: Guidelines for Beneficial Use of AI in Research
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: For administrative activities, the University of Milan says its AI adoption follows AGID public-administration guidelines and uses a methodology that considers AI Act compliance, data quality, personal data protection and cybersecurity requirements.

citation.citationTitle: Guidelines for Beneficial Use of AI in Administrative Activities
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Guidelines for Beneficial Use of AI in Administrative Activities
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: As a public university, Universita degli Studi di Milano... promotes the adoption of AI tools in compliance with AGID guidelines for AI adoption in Public Administration. The following categories of requirements are particularly taken into consideration in the analysis phase: AI Act compliance; data management and quality; personal data protection; cybersecurity, in compliance with AGID guidelines.
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

citation.citationTitle: Guidelines for Beneficial Use of AI in Administrative Activities
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.notes: Approved as a narrow claim about the existence and purpose of the official AI governance document and annexes.
Approved because the education guidelines directly state that teacher and student AI use must be explicit and declared in the described contexts.
Approved because the education guidelines directly distinguish permitted explicit self-assessment from non-permitted delegation of final-grade assessment to AI tools.
Approved as scoped to research guidance and phrased as advice/responsibility rather than a broad binding prohibition.
Approved because the research guidelines directly discuss data protection, traceability, regulatory compliance, and avoiding particularly sensitive data in AI systems.
Approved as a narrow administrative-activities claim based on the official administrative guidelines.

artifacts.title: University of Milan AI Guidance Staged Findings
artifacts.summary: Official University of Milan sources describe a university AI governance document and official guideline annexes for education, research, and administrative activities. Staged claims are limited to AI governance purpose, AI-use declaration in education, assessment limits, research responsibility/transparency, research data protection, and administrative AGID-aligned adoption methodology.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.

The English PDF annexes state they were translated from original Italian using AI and reviewed by qualified human staff; this bundle uses the official English versions fetched from the University domain.
No login-gated, department-only, vendor, social media, news-only, or repository scholarship source was used as claim evidence.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
